Description:en: Occiput Anterior indicates that the baby is coming down the birth canal, head down, with the posterior of the head coming under the pelvic bone and the baby’s face facing the mother’s sacrum.
Fetal position during labor where the back of the baby’s head (occiput) is toward the mother’s back (posterior).
Is the passage of a small amount of blood-tinged mucus from the cervix through the vagina.
Refers to any observable or palpable result obtained during a pelvic examination.
Engagement of the baby, in free text
Question to record cervix examination in text
Question to record status of the fetal membrane

Fully specified name:en: LABOR DURATION IN MINUTES, ACTIVE STAGE
Description:en: Question to answer the length of time in minutes that labor took in the active stage.

Fully specified name:en: LABOR DURATION IN HOURS, SECOND STAGE
Description:en: Question to answer the length of time in hours that labor took in the second stage.
